name: Mina
context: high-school senior; threads: college entrance prep, rescue cat Mochi, art-club mural

episodes.text: First day back at school after the break. I organized my desk, copied the new timetable into my planner, and grabbed a window seat before anyone else claimed it.

probes.query: Which seat did I grab on the first day back at school?
I feel calm and content thinking back to how that semester started.
The way that term opened still drifts through my head now and then.

episodes.text: We finally brought the rescue cat home today. She hid inside a shoe box for an hour, then curled up on my lap, and I was so happy I could barely breathe.

probes.query: Where did the rescue cat hide when she first came home?
I still feel overjoyed remembering the day my cat arrived.
That evening a tiny creature chose to stay near me, and it keeps coming back to my mind.

episodes.text: Spent Sunday drafting the mural concept for art club. Three sketches of a whale drifting over rooftops; the third one finally looked the way I imagined.

probes.query: What animal did I sketch for the art club mural?
I feel quietly proud of how that design work turned out.
There was a weekend when an idea slowly took shape under my pencil, and I return to it.

episodes.text: Bought new highlighters and index cards at the stationery store, then reorganized my study binder by subject. Nothing exciting, just housekeeping for exam prep.

probes.query: What did I buy at the stationery store?
I feel calm about how I set up my study materials back then.
That little bit of tidying before the busy season crosses my mind sometimes.

episodes.text: My mock exam score dropped two grades in math. I sat in the bathroom stall at lunch and cried, then couldn't focus for the rest of the day.

probes.query: Which subject dropped two grades on my mock exam?
I still feel crushed and anxious when that test result comes to mind.
There was a number on a page that undid me for a whole afternoon, and it visits me before every big day.

episodes.text: Mochi knocked a glass of water onto my homework and I had to recopy two pages of notes before bed. Annoying, but the notes came out neater the second time.

probes.query: What did Mochi spill on my homework?
I get mildly annoyed remembering what the cat did to my notes that night.
A small domestic disaster made me redo an evening's work once, and I think of it while studying.

episodes.text: Our team painted the base coat of the mural on the gym wall. Twelve of us in old shirts, blue paint everywhere, someone's speaker playing city pop the whole time.

probes.query: What music was playing while we painted the gym wall?
I feel so happy whenever I remember painting that wall with everyone.
One loud, messy afternoon with my whole crew still glows in my memory.

episodes.text: Weekly library session with Dayeon. We split a cinnamon roll, quizzed each other on English vocab for two hours, and left when the closing announcement played.

probes.query: What snack did Dayeon and I split at the library?
I feel content thinking about those steady study sessions with my friend.
Those quiet evenings of practice with a friend drift back to me during busy weeks.

episodes.text: Grandma called and we talked for an hour about her garden and my exams. Before hanging up she said she keeps my childhood drawing taped inside her cupboard, and I teared up.

probes.query: What does Grandma keep taped inside her cupboard?
That phone call with Grandma still makes me feel loved and a little teary.
Something an elder told me on the phone one night settled deep and stays warm.

episodes.text: Argued with Mom about cram school hours. She wants me to add Sunday classes and I shouted that I never get a single day off, then slammed my door.

probes.query: What did Mom want me to add that started our argument?
I still feel angry and guilty about that fight with Mom.
Something I shut too hard between me and someone at home echoes in my head.

episodes.text: Took Mochi to the vet for her booster shot. She yowled through the waiting room, weighed in at four kilograms, and sulked in her carrier the whole ride back.

probes.query: How much did Mochi weigh at the vet?
I feel calm remembering that routine trip for the cat's shot.
That noisy little errand with a grumpy passenger pops into my head at odd times.

episodes.text: The finished mural was unveiled at the school festival and the whole hallway clapped. My whale, huge and blue, with every club member's signature hidden in the waves.

probes.query: What is hidden in the waves of the finished mural?
I feel proud beyond words about the day our artwork was revealed.
A corridor of applause for a thing we made together keeps replaying in me.

episodes.text: Reviewed two practice passages, fed Mochi, and went to bed early. Set out tomorrow's uniform so the morning would be easier.

probes.query: What did I set out before bed to make the morning easier?
I feel calm about that quiet weeknight of small chores.
One plain school-night wind-down floats back to me when things get loud.

episodes.text: Dayeon texted that she might transfer schools before the final semester. I reread the message five times and felt something heavy settle in my chest.

probes.query: What did Dayeon say she might do before the final semester?
I feel worried and heavy-hearted about the news my friend texted me.
A few lines on a screen from someone dear changed the weather in me, and I keep going over them in my head.
